jobs.title: Data Analyst (Entry-Level / Junior)
jobs.description:
Data Analyst (Entry-Level / Junior)

Role Description Support business and operational decision-making by collecting, organizing, analyzing, and interpreting data from multiple sources to generate meaningful insights and recommendations. Develop and maintain reports, dashboards, and data visualizations that provide stakeholders with clear visibility into business performance, operational trends, customer behavior, and key performance indicators. Perform data validation, cleansing, and quality assurance activities to ensure the accuracy, consistency, and reliability of datasets. Analyze large datasets to identify patterns, opportunities, risks, and areas for improvement, and communicate findings through clear reports and presentations. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to support forecasting, performance measurement, process optimization, and strategic initiatives that contribute to organizational growth and operational excellence.
Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, Business Administration, Computer Science, Information Systems, Engineering, or a related field. Strong analytical, critical-thinking, and problem-solving abilities with exceptional attention to detail. Proficiency in spreadsheets, data analysis tools, reporting platforms, and data visualization software. Familiarity with SQL, database concepts, statistical methodologies, and data management practices is advantageous. Excellent communication and presentation skills with the ability to translate complex data into clear and actionable insights for both technical and non-technical stakeholders. Strong organizational and time-management capabilities with the ability to manage multiple priorities and deadlines effectively. Knowledge of business intelligence, reporting frameworks, data governance, forecasting, performance measurement, and process improvement principles is highly beneficial.

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Entry level

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Information Technology

Industries

Broadcast Media Production and Distribution

jobs.title: Data Analyst – Palantir Foundry - Must be government clearance eligible
jobs.description:
About Navaide

Navaide partners with federal clients to deliver mission-focused solutions across financial management, ERP, and digital transformation. We bring deep domain expertise, practical execution, and a people-first culture to complex government environments.

Catalysts for a Stronger Tomorrow

At Navaide, we exist to empower organizations to evolve and adapt in a rapidly changing world. By combining human ingenuity with transformative technology, we develop innovative solutions that drive progress for the people and systems that strengthen our nation. Our mission is rooted in agility, scalability, and a commitment to exceeding expectations, ensuring impactful results for our clients and communities.

How You Will Make an Impact

We are seeking a talented and motivated Data Analyst to provide critical support to the DOW contract. The successful candidate will play a pivotal role in data analysis and visualization using tools, while also acquiring expertise in Palantir Foundry platforms. Databricks experience is required.

Key Duties & Responsibilities

Conduct data analysis to derive actionable insights and recommendations to support the DOW’s contract’s objectives.
Utilize Tableau and Foundry applications, including Contour and Quiver, to create data visualizations, dashboards, and reports to facilitate decision-making processes.
Familiarity with the Foundry instance within the War Data Platform and Palantir Foundry platforms, collaborating with team members who possess experience in these systems.
Work with different data sources, ensuring data is accurately collected, transformed, and loaded for analysis.
Clean and preprocess data to eliminate inconsistencies, errors, and outliers.
Employ statistical techniques to interpret and validate data trends, patterns, and anomalies.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to understand project requirements and deliver insights that support the contract’s objectives.
Prepare and deliver reports and presentations that effectively communicate analytical findings to non-technical stakeholders.
Adhere to data security and privacy protocols, including Palantir’s privacy and civil liberty (PCL) principles, ensuring the confidentiality and integrity of sensitive information.

Relevant Skills & Experience

Proven experience with data analysis and visualization tools, including Palantir Foundry applications such as Contour, Quiver, and Object Explorer.
Willingness to learn and adapt to new technologies
Strong analytical skills, attention to detail, and the ability to draw meaningful conclusions from data.
Familiarity with big data platforms (e.g., Databricks); familiarity with Palantir Foundry’s data integration and ontology (data model) capabilities is a plus.
Relevant experience in data cleaning, transformation, and statistical analysis.
Excellent communication and presentation skills to convey complex data insights to non-technical stakeholders.
Ability to work collaboratively in a team-oriented environment.
Strong problem-solving skills and a proactive approach to identifying and addressing data-related challenges.
Ability to obtain an U.S. Government Security Clearance

Travel

Up to 10% travel, depending on environment cutovers, stakeholder design sessions, and validation/test events.

Education

Bachelor’s Degree from accredited college or university in Accounting, Finance, Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, or Information Systems.
Palantir Foundry Data Analyst Certification is a plus.

What We Offer

Navaide is committed to fostering a supportive, inclusive, and forward-thinking workplace where our team members can thrive. Our benefits are designed to support your well-being, professional growth, and ability to work at a sustainable pace.

Competitive compensation: $78,000 - $145,000/ year (commensurate with experience)
Comprehensive benefits, including medical, dental, and vision coverage
Flexible time off
Professional development opportunities
Company perks such as flex spending, wellness initiatives, etc.
401(K) matching

About Our Culture

Navaide honors integrity, curiosity, diligence, and collaboration in everything we do. We believe in building strong relationships with our clients, communities, and teammates, united by a shared purpose of creating a stronger tomorrow. Navaide is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity employer.

Ready to Make a Difference?

For more about us, please check out the following links:

About Navaide

Other Opportunities

Employee Benefits

Connect with us on LinkedIn!

Equal Opportunity Employer: Navaide is an EEO Employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

E-Verify Participation:

E-Verify Participation Poster

IER Right to Work Poster

No third parties, please.

jobs.title: Project Coordinator
jobs.description:
Overview

We are seeking a detail‐oriented Project Coordinator
to support high‐volume energy efficiency and demand response programs
. This role focuses on program administration, rebate processing, data validation, and operational support across multiple initiatives.

You will work in a fast‐paced, metrics‐driven environment
, managing large volumes of applications and supporting program accuracy and compliance. This is an excellent opportunity for early‐career professionals interested in utilities, sustainability, or energy programs
.

Responsibilities

Process high‐volume rebate applications and work orders with a strong focus on accuracy and compliance
Validate customer eligibility, equipment requirements, and incentive amounts
Review applications submitted via digital systems, PDFs, and physical documentation
Manage and prioritize workload from shared system queues
Track progress using dashboards and meet performance metrics and quotas
Collaborate with internal teams and third‐party vendors to resolve issues
Support both residential and commercial program workflows as needed
Assist with project tracking, documentation, and reporting activities
Prepare status reports, summaries, and presentations for stakeholders
Maintain organized documentation and data records
Contribute to process improvements and operational efficiency

Required Qualifications

1–3 years of experience in a high‐volume operational or administrative environment
Strong attention to detail and ability to validate large amounts of data
Experience with data entry, program processing, or operational workflows
Ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines in a fast‐paced setting
Strong analytical and problem‐solving skills
Proficiency with Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint)
Excellent verbal and written communication skills
Self‐driven with strong accountability and work ethic (especially in remote settings)

Preferred Qualifications

Experience with Salesforce or similar systems
Background in utility programs, rebates, or regulated environments
Familiarity with Excel functions and data analysis
Experience working with PDFs, document processing, or scanning tools
Interest in energy efficiency, sustainability, or utility operations

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Mid-Senior level

Employment type

Contract

Job function

Other

Industries

IT Services and IT Consulting, Accounting, and Financial Services

jobs.title: MIS Product Configurator and Data Analyst
jobs.description:
Job Title:
MIS Product Configurator and Data Analyst
Location:
On Site - Los Angeles, California, United States
Salary:
$75000-$130000
Skills:
CIS Configurator, SAP Business One, SQL, Manufacturing BOMs, Data Modeling

About the Manufacturing Company / The Opportunity:

Join an innovative leader in the building supply manufacturing industry that is modernizing its production and data platforms. This is a pivotal opportunity to architect and optimize the translation of complex door configurations into seamless system logic, working alongside skilled teams at the forefront of technology transformation. Make a direct impact on efficiency and product delivery by shaping advanced ERP and manufacturing systems in a collaborative, growth-focused environment.

Responsibilities:

Translate manufacturing requirements and sales/pricing rules into precise system logic using the CIS Configurator for a diverse range of SKUs and entry door products.
Develop and maintain advanced configuration models, dimensional and handing logic, and dependency rules for components, pricing structures, and compatibility mapping.
Oversee item masters and multi-level Bills of Materials (BOMs) in SAP Business One, ensuring execution layer data integrity and “single source of truth” for production and procurement processes.
Define operation sequences, maintain standard routing data, and ensure detailed scheduling for on-time delivery within MES and APS systems (LYNQ, WiSys).
Lead system validation, User Acceptance Testing (UAT), and proactively audit order-to-cash process flows to guarantee error-free production.
Extract legacy knowledge from legacy systems (AS/400 Friedman), translating manual processes into standardized, automated solutions for SAP B1 and CIS.
Utilize SQL and advanced Excel for margin validation, root-cause analysis, and ongoing auditing of production schedule accuracy.
Support, monitor, and troubleshoot EDI and API-based integrations connecting core platforms (ERP, MES, WMS), and maintain Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) for configuration logic.

Must-Have Skills:

Minimum 5 years’ experience in manufacturing systems, with emphasis on product structures, multi-level Bills of Materials (BOMs), and routings.
Hands-on expertise with CIS Configurator or similar rules-based CPQ software, and proficiency in SAP Business One ERP.
Strong SQL skills for data modeling, technical troubleshooting, and cross-platform integration.
Advanced Excel skills for auditing and margin analysis.
Proven ability to translate manufacturing and sales requirements into system logic and configuration models.
Exceptional attention to detail and a “zero-error” approach to data entry and systems maintenance.
Effective communicator with experience bridging Sales, Engineering, and Shop-Floor Operations.

Nice-to-Have Skills:

Experience supporting or monitoring EDI, API-based integrations, or middleware between ERP and MES systems.
Familiarity with the AS/400 Friedman system or legacy-to-modern system migration projects.
Direct experience configuring, maintaining, or integrating LYNQ MES and WiSys WMS platforms.
Background in documenting technical knowledge and developing SOPs for complex manufacturing rules.
Exposure to discrete manufacturing environments involving highly customizable or configured-to-order products.

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Mid-Senior level

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Analyst and Manufacturing

Industries

Furniture and Home Furnishings Manufacturing

jobs.title: Project Data Analyst (Entry / Junior Level)
jobs.description:
Project Data Analyst (Entry / Junior Level)

Role Description Support project teams by collecting, organizing, analyzing, and interpreting data to improve project planning, execution, and overall performance. Develop and maintain reports, dashboards, and data visualizations that provide stakeholders with actionable insights into project schedules, budgets, resource allocation, risks, and key performance indicators. Monitor project metrics, identify trends, evaluate project outcomes, and recommend opportunities for process optimization and operational improvement. Assist with forecasting, performance tracking, data validation, and reporting activities to ensure the accuracy, consistency, and reliability of project information. Collaborate with project managers and cross-functional teams to support data-driven decision-making, enhance operational efficiency, and contribute to the successful delivery of projects and strategic initiatives across the organization. Support project governance activities by ensuring accurate reporting, documentation, and performance monitoring throughout the project lifecycle.
Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Business Administration, Economics, Information Systems, Engineering, or a related field. Strong analytical, critical-thinking, and problem-solving abilities with exceptional attention to detail. Proficiency in spreadsheets, reporting tools, data analysis methodologies, and data visualization platforms. Familiarity with SQL, database concepts, statistical techniques, project performance measurement, and business reporting practices is advantageous. Excellent communication and presentation skills with the ability to translate complex data into meaningful business insights for both technical and non-technical stakeholders. Strong organizational and time-management capabilities with the ability to manage multiple priorities and deadlines effectively. Knowledge of forecasting, process improvement methodologies, project management principles, business intelligence concepts, and performance analytics is highly beneficial.

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Mid-Senior level

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Information Technology

Industries

Online Audio and Video Media

jobs.title: Youth Project Coordinator
jobs.description:
Job Responsibilities

Youth Project Coordinator

Department:
LAW Children's Law Center

Part/Full Time:
Full Time

Advertised Salary Range

About UofSC

From the Upstate to the Lowcountry, the University of South Carolina system is transforming the lives of South Carolinians through the impact of our eight institutions and 20 locations throughout the state. More than 50,000 students are enrolled at one of eight institutions, including the research campus in Columbia and comprehensive four-year universities in Aiken, Upstate and Beaufort. In addition, our Palmetto College campuses in Salkehatchie, Union, Lancaster and Sumter enable students to earn associate or bachelor’s degrees through a combination of in-person, online or blended learning. All of our system institutions place strong emphasis on service — helping to build healthier, more educated communities in South Carolina and beyond.

Advertised Job Summary

Are you passionate about building safer communities and creating meaningful changes for youth and families? Do you thrive on bringing people together, facilitating dialogue, and turning big ideas into action? If so, we invite you to join our team as the Youth Project Coordinator for the Safeguarding Our Youth: A Collaborative Blueprint for Deflection and Recovery initiative.

This full-time position is based in Chester, SC, and serves the counties of Chester, Fairfield, and Lancaster. In this role, you will lead and coordinate impactful grant activities designed to reduce youth violence, prevent system involvement, and promote youth well-being through early intervention and community-based strategies.

As the Youth Project Coordinator, you will collaborate with a diverse network of stakeholders, including:

Educators and school leaders
Law enforcement agencies
Child welfare and juvenile justice professionals
Mental and behavioral health providers
Peer support specialists
Individuals with lived experience
Local and county leaders
Public and private service providers
Youth and families

The ideal candidate is energized by community engagement and relationship-building. You must enjoy traveling throughout the three counties, getting to know local partners, and serving as a connector across systems. This position requires someone who is comfortable facilitating dialogue among diverse groups, coordinating community events, leading community and stakeholder meetings, and fostering collaboration across agencies.

Key Responsibilities Will Include

Coordinating and implementing grant-funded initiatives
Leading and facilitating cross-sector meetings and discussions
Organizing and managing community trainings and events
Strengthening partnerships across multiple counties
Working closely with the Program Manager and Project Assistant to ensure successful program delivery
Supporting data collection and reporting related to grant outcomes

This is an exciting opportunity for a motivated professional who believes in prevention, values collaboration, and is committed to uplifting youth and families. If you are a relationship-builder, a strategic thinker, and someone who enjoys being out in the community making things happen, we encourage you to apply.

Join us in shaping safer, stronger futures for youth across Chester, Fairfield, and Lancaster Counties.

Preferred Qualifications

Required Education and Experience

Prefer work experience that involves multi-agency coordination.
Strongly prefer training experience. Prefer experience in grants management and program development.
Prefer professional licensure in South Carolina.

Knowledge/Skills/Abilities

This position requires a proactive and knowledgeable professional with a strong understanding of the systems serving children and youth in South Carolina, including juvenile justice, child welfare, behavioral health, education, and community-based services.
The ideal candidate values prevention, early intervention, and community-based treatment approaches, recognizing that youth are best served in their homes and communities whenever they are not a threat of harm to themselves or others.
Strong communication, planning, and organizational skills are essential for effectively managing multiple projects and coordinating activities across three counties.
The successful candidate excels in relationship-building, fostering cross-sector collaboration, and maintaining positive public relations.
They are confident in leading workshops, facilitating dialogue among diverse stakeholders, and navigating complex systems with professionalism and cultural humility.
A background in youth development, welfare, health, behavioral health, or related fields is highly desirable.
A strong commitment to helping underserved communities connect to needed resources and services is essential.

Job Close Date

06/30/2026

Special Instructions to Applicant

Positions are advertised for a minimum of five (5) business days on our job website. After five (5) business days, positions can be closed at the discretion of the department at any time. This employment site is updated on a regular basis. The length of the recruitment and screening process may vary from position to position, depending upon a variety of factors. Should review of your qualifications result in a decision to pursue your candidacy, you will be contacted by phone or email. We are only accepting applications submitted by June 30, 2026.

To apply, please visit: https://apptrkr.com/7242381

EEO Statement

The University of South Carolina does not discriminate in educational or employment opportunities on the basis of race, sex, gender, gender identity, transgender status, age, color, religion, national origin, disability, sexual orientation, genetics, protected veteran status, pregnancy, childbirth or related medical conditions.

Copyright ©2025 Jobelephant.com Inc. All rights reserved.

https://www.jobelephant.com/

About UofSC

From the Upstate to the Lowcountry, the University of South Carolina system is transforming the lives of South Carolinians through the impact of our eight institutions and 20 locations throughout the state. More than 50,000 students are enrolled at one of eight institutions, including the research campus in Columbia and comprehensive four-year universities in Aiken, Upstate and Beaufort. In addition, our Palmetto College campuses in Salkehatchie, Union, Lancaster and Sumter enable students to earn associate or bachelor’s degrees through a combination of in-person, online or blended learning. All of our system institutions place strong emphasis on service — helping to build healthier, more educated communities in South Carolina and beyond.

Advertised Job Summary

Are you passionate about building safer communities and creating meaningful changes for youth and families? Do you thrive on bringing people together, facilitating dialogue, and turning big ideas into action? If so, we invite you to join our team as the Youth Project Coordinator for the Safeguarding Our Youth: A Collaborative Blueprint for Deflection and Recovery initiative.

This full-time position is based in Chester, SC, and serves the counties of Chester, Fairfield, and Lancaster. In this role, you will lead and coordinate impactful grant activities designed to reduce youth violence, prevent system involvement, and promote youth well-being through early intervention and community-based strategies.

As the Youth Project Coordinator, you will collaborate with a diverse network of stakeholders, including:

Educators and school leaders
Law enforcement agencies
Child welfare and juvenile justice professionals
Mental and behavioral health providers
Peer support specialists
Individuals with lived experience
Local and county leaders
Public and private service providers
Youth and families

The ideal candidate is energized by community engagement and relationship-building. You must enjoy traveling throughout the three counties, getting to know local partners, and serving as a connector across systems. This position requires someone who is comfortable facilitating dialogue among diverse groups, coordinating community events, leading community and stakeholder meetings, and fostering collaboration across agencies.

Key Responsibilities Will Include

Coordinating and implementing grant-funded initiatives
Leading and facilitating cross-sector meetings and discussions
Organizing and managing community trainings and events
Strengthening partnerships across multiple counties
Working closely with the Program Manager and Project Assistant to ensure successful program delivery
Supporting data collection and reporting related to grant outcomes

This is an exciting opportunity for a motivated professional who believes in prevention, values collaboration, and is committed to uplifting youth and families. If you are a relationship-builder, a strategic thinker, and someone who enjoys being out in the community making things happen, we encourage you to apply.

Join us in shaping safer, stronger futures for youth across Chester, Fairfield, and Lancaster Counties.

Preferred Qualifications

Required Education and Experience

Prefer work experience that involves multi-agency coordination.
Strongly prefer training experience. Prefer experience in grants management and program development.
Prefer professional licensure in South Carolina.

Knowledge/Skills/Abilities

This position requires a proactive and knowledgeable professional with a strong understanding of the systems serving children and youth in South Carolina, including juvenile justice, child welfare, behavioral health, education, and community-based services.
The ideal candidate values prevention, early intervention, and community-based treatment approaches, recognizing that youth are best served in their homes and communities whenever they are not a threat of harm to themselves or others.
Strong communication, planning, and organizational skills are essential for effectively managing multiple projects and coordinating activities across three counties.
The successful candidate excels in relationship-building, fostering cross-sector collaboration, and maintaining positive public relations.
They are confident in leading workshops, facilitating dialogue among diverse stakeholders, and navigating complex systems with professionalism and cultural humility.
A background in youth development, welfare, health, behavioral health, or related fields is highly desirable.
A strong commitment to helping underserved communities connect to needed resources and services is essential.

06/30/2026
